title: Plans for the 2025 Bluefin tuna (BFT) catch and release recreational fisheries (CRRF) within UK waters
body: " 13 October 2025  Defra, MMO and sector representatives are working together to develop and introduce mandatory training as soon as possible. At this time, we are unable to confirm what training will be required going forward but we will publish details as soon as possible.    Friday 27 June 2025 - Key update  The application process has closed. Applicants will be notified by email whether they have been successful or not early next week, the week commencing the 30 June.  If you do not receive an email from the MMO regarding the application process outcome next week (wc 30 June), please enquire at  BFT.Recreational@marinemanagement.org.uk   Home Nations  England  The Marine Management Organisation (MMO) will open a permitted BFT catch and release recreational fishery (CRRF) in 2025 within English waters.  The MMO will proceed with the application process that will allow approximately 140 permitted vessels to operate in the BFT CRRF in English waters.  The authorised fishery is scheduled to open from mid-July and will run until the end of November 2025.  The revised key dates for the English 2025 BFT CRRF are:   6 May 2025 - BFT CRRF application period opens. (Previously set as 16 April) 2 June 2025 - BFT CRRF application period closes. (Previously set as 18 May) Applicants will now be notified whether they have been successful or not early next week, the week commencing the 30 June. No later than 13 July 2025 - BFT CRRF permits issued to applicants. (Unaffected) 18 July 2025 - BFT CRRF permit season opens. (Unaffected) 30 November 2025 -BFT CRRF permit season closes. (Unaffected)   Wales  Welsh Government also plan to open their bluefin tuna catch and release recreational fishery in Welsh waters. Further details can be found at the following link:  Bluefin Tuna Catch and Release Recreational Fishery GOV.WALES  Jersey  Government of Jersey are proposing to open their bluefin tuna catch and release charter fishery in Jersey waters. For further information, please contact the Government of Jersey’s Marine Resources department on marineresources@gov.je  Scotland, Northern Ireland and other Crown Dependencies  Scotland, Northern Ireland, Isle of Man and Guernsey governments are not proposing to open a BFT CRRF in their waters this year.   Joining the 2025 English waters BFT CRRF.  You will need to apply for a permit from the MMO to join this fishery  The MMO will be responsible for issuing permits for the English BFT CRRF.  In 2025, permits will be free of charge, however in future years, charges are likely to be introduced.  Permit numbers will be proportionate to the amount of quota available to cover any incidental BFT mortalities.  Those issued with a BFT CRRF permit MUST comply and report their fishing activity in line with the following legislation: The Sea Fisheries - International Commission for the Conservation of Atlantic Tunas (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2024.  How the English waters BFT CRRF permits will be allocated  The BFT CRRF application process, to be considered for a permit, will be open from 6 May until 23:59 on 2 June 2025.  To...
